context: Tests that the agent replaces bare 'make' calls with '$(MAKE)', adds .ONESHELL for multi-step shell recipe reliability, and explains the parallel build jobserver issue.

checklist.name: $(MAKE) in build target
checklist.description: The corrected Makefile's build target uses $(MAKE) instead of bare 'make' for recursive sub-project calls

checklist.name: $(MAKE) in test target
checklist.description: The corrected Makefile's test target uses $(MAKE) instead of bare 'make'

checklist.name: $(MAKE) in deploy target
checklist.description: The corrected Makefile's deploy target uses $(MAKE) instead of bare 'make'

checklist.name: $(MAKE) in clean target
checklist.description: The corrected Makefile's clean target uses $(MAKE) instead of bare 'make'

checklist.name: Bare make risk explained
checklist.description: fix-notes.md explains that bare 'make' does not inherit jobserver flags and breaks parallel builds with -j

checklist.name: .ONESHELL or equivalent addressed
checklist.description: The corrected Makefile adds .ONESHELL OR fix-notes.md explains that .ONESHELL would prevent the multi-step script from continuing past failures

checklist.name: .ONESHELL benefit explained
checklist.description: fix-notes.md explains how .ONESHELL affects multi-step recipe error handling

checklist.name: All recursive calls fixed
checklist.description: All four targets (build, test, deploy, clean) that contained bare 'make -C' calls are updated to '$(MAKE) -C'
